title: 10 best herbal teas for dry lips
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry lips are infusions made from various plants and herbs that, when consumed, can help alleviate dry and chapped lips. These teas work by hydrating the body from the inside out, providing essential moisture that helps keep lips soft and supple. Examples of herbal teas that can help with dry lips include chamomile, rosehip, and lavender teas. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked lips, while rosehip tea is rich in vitamin C and antioxidants that can help repair and protect lips from environmental stressors. Lavender tea has calming and moisturizing properties that can help keep lips hydrated and comfortable. By drinking these herbal teas, people can improve their overall lip health, leading to softer, smoother, and more moisturized lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with dry lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and emollient properties that help soothe and moisturize chapped lips. The tea is rich in antioxidants and vitamins, including vitamin A and E, which are known to promote skin health and hydration. Drinking pot marigold tea can help keep your body hydrated, which in turn can help prevent dry lips. Additionally, applying the tea topically to the lips can create a protective barrier that helps lock in moisture and shield the lips from environmental stressors. These benefits make pot marigold tea an effective natural remedy for dry and chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Pot marigold tea contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, which help to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Healing: The tea is rich in wound-healing properties due to the presence of the active constituent, pro-vitamin A, which promotes cell regeneration and assists in the healing of dry, cracked lips.
Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea has potent anti-inflammatory properties, primarily due to the flavonoid content, which aids in reducing redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry lips.
Antibacterial: The tea contains antibacterial properties, mainly attributed to its flavonoid and essential oil content, that help protect dry lips from infection and further irritation.
Soothing: Pot marigold tea has a calming effect on dry lips, thanks to its demulcent properties, which create a protective barrier and help to alleviate discomfort and itchiness caused by dryness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and emollient properties, flowers help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing high levels of vitamins and minerals, leaves aid in healing and nourishing dry, cracked lips.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds help to lock in moisture and protect lips from environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, place 1 cup of dried marigold flowers.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the marigold flowers.
Let the marigold flowers steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the marigold flowers from the water.
Drink the marigold tea 2-3 times a day for dry lip relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with dry lips because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and protect the lips from environmental stressors. The tea's high antioxidant content, including EGCG, helps to repair damaged skin cells and promotes the production of collagen, which keeps the lips soft and supple. Additionally, white tea has a natural moisturizing effect, as it contains high levels of vitamin E and essential fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and prevent dryness. Regular use of white tea can help to keep the lips hydrated and nourished, reducing the appearance of dry, chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation of dry, chapped lips. This property is important for dry lips because it helps soothe and calm irritated skin. The active constituents that give white tea its anti-inflammatory properties include tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: White tea is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as pollution and UV radiation. This property is important for dry lips because it helps prevent further damage and keeps the lips healthy. The active constituents that give white tea its antioxidant properties include polyphenols, catechins, and epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG).

Moisturizing: White tea has moisturizing properties that can help hydrate and soften dry, chapped lips. This property is important for dry lips because it helps restore the lips' natural moisture barrier. The active constituents that give white tea its moisturizing properties include amino acids, vitamins, and minerals.

Antibacterial: White tea has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infection and promote wound healing. This property is important for dry lips because it helps prevent further irritation and promotes healthy lip tissue. The active constituents that give white tea its antibacterial properties include flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils.

Soothing: White tea has soothing properties that can help calm and relax dry, chapped lips. This property is important for dry lips because it helps alleviate discomfort and provides relief. The active constituents that give white tea its soothing properties include flavonoids, tannins, and volatile compounds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that can help to soothe and heal dry, chapped lips.
Buds: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, buds can help to moisturize and protect the lips from further damage.
Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation on dry, cracked lips.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, seeds can help to nourish and hydrate the lips, providing long-lasting relief from dryness.
Bark: Contains tannins that can help to tighten and protect the skin on the lips, preventing further moisture loss.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and let 2 teaspoons of dried white tea leaves steep for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and stir until fully dissolved.
Mix in 1/2 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and let the mixture steep for an additional 3 minutes.
Strain the tea and allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a clean washcloth in the tea and apply it to dry lips for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with dry lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate chapped lips. The tea is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that can help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage. Additionally, the emollient properties of German chamomile tea can help to lock in moisture, preventing further dryness and cracking. Drinking chamomile tea can also help to keep the body hydrated, which can contribute to overall lip health. Regularly applying chamomile tea to the lips can provide relief from dryness and promote soft, supple lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea helps reduce inflammation and swelling, which can soothe dry, chapped lips. This property is important because it alleviates irritation and promotes healing. The active constituents responsible for this property are the flavonoids and terpenoids found in the tea.

Moisturizing: German chamomile tea has moisturizing properties that help lock in moisture and soothe dry lips. This property is important because it prevents further dryness and cracking. The moisturizing effect comes from the humectant properties of the tea.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea contains antioxidants that help protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can cause dryness and damage. This property is important because it helps maintain healthy lip tissue and prevents premature aging. The antioxidants in the tea come from its high flavonoid content.

Antimicrobial: German chamomile tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infections and promote wound healing. This property is important because it helps prevent further irritation and promotes healthy lip tissue. The antimicrobial properties come from the volatile oils and flavonoids found in the tea.

Analgesic: German chamomile tea has analgesic properties that help soothe pain and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips. This property is important because it provides relief and promotes healing. The analgesic properties come from the flavonoids and volatile oils found in the tea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in moisturizing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, chamomile leaves aid in healing and softening dry, cracked lips.
Seeds: With a high content of nourishing fatty acids, chamomile seeds help to lock in moisture and protect lips from dryness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the flowers.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain the tea.
Drink the tea or use it as a lip soak for 10-15 minutes, then rinse off.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tea helps with dry lips because it contains a variety of natural herbs that are known to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips. The tea includes ingredients such as chamomile, rosehip, and hibiscus, which are all rich in antioxidants and vitamins that can help to heal and protect the lips. Additionally, the tea may also help to increase hydration in the body, which can help to prevent dry lips in the first place. Drinking Eureka tea regularly can provide long-term relief from dry lips, while also offering a range of other health benefits.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Eureka tea contains high levels of antioxidants and vitamins, such as Vitamin E, which help to hydrate and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: Eureka tea has anti-inflammatory properties, due to its high content of polyphenols and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation of dry, cracked lips.
Healing: Eureka tea has been shown to promote wound healing, thanks to its rich antioxidant profile, which can help speed up the recovery of dry, chapped lips.
Soothing: Eureka tea has a soothing effect on dry, chapped lips, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate discomfort and provide relief.

Protective: Eureka tea has been found to have protective properties, thanks to its high levels of antioxidants, which can help shield lips from environmental stressors, such as wind, sun, and cold, that can contribute to dry lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, leaves help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Buds: High in vitamins and minerals, buds provide nourishment and promote lip tissue regeneration.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, flowers help reduce redness and irritation caused by dry lips.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids, seeds help lock in moisture and protect lips from environmental stressors.
Rhyzomes: Abundant in nutrients and phytochemicals, rhyzomes aid in healing and rejuvenating dry, damaged lips.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of dried chamomile flowers, 1/2 cup of dried lemon balm leaves, and 2 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 teaspoon of almond oil into the tea.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with dry lips because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and heal dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and promoting the repair of damaged tissue. Additionally, the tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that help to moisturize and nourish the lips, preventing further dryness and cracking. The herbal tea can be applied directly to the lips or consumed as a beverage to provide hydration and promote overall lip health.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective film on the lips, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dryness.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of phenolic compounds, which help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with dry, chapped lips.
Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote tissue repair.
Antimicrobial: The tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, thanks to the presence of alkaloids and saponins, which help prevent lip infections and promote faster healing of dry, cracked lips.
Emollient: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains emollient compounds, such as fatty acids, that help soften and smooth dry, rough lips, providing long-lasting hydration and comfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the flowers help soothe and heal dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and vitamins, the leaves provide moisturizing and nourishing benefits for dry lips.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and nutrients, the seeds help to lock in moisture and protect the lips from dryness and environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 2 cups of water for 5-7 minutes.
Meanwhile, crush 2 tablespoons of dried eastern purple coneflower petals in a mortar and pestle.
Place the crushed petals in a teapot and pour the boiling water over them.
Steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain out the petals.
Drink the tea while it's warm, 2-3 times a day for dry lip relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with dry lips because it contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal chapped lips. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia. The tree's leaves are rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and other compounds that can help to moisturize and protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as wind, sun, and cold. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea can help to hydrate the body from the inside out, which can also help to keep the lips soft and supple. Additionally, applying the tea directly to the lips as a compress or using it as a lip balm can provide instant relief and nourishment to dry, cracked lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea's anti-inflammatory property, derived from its high polyphenol content, helps soothe dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and irritation.
Emollient: The emollient effect of Tasmanian Blue Gum tea, due to its essential oils, helps lock in moisture, providing relief for dry, cracked lips.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property, attributed to its flavonoids and tannins, helps protect the lips from environmental stressors that can lead to dryness and damage.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property, owing to the presence of terpenes and sesquiterpenes, helps prevent lip infections and further dryness that can exacerbate dry lip conditions.
Vasodilation: The vasodilation effect of Tasmanian Blue Gum tea, caused by its volatile oils, improves blood flow to the lips, promoting faster healing and hydration of dry, flaky lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe dry and chapped lips.
Bark: Rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help moisturize and protect dry lips.
Flowers: Contain essential oils and flavonoids, which can help nourish and hydrate dry lips.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, which can help moisturize and protect dry lips.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to moisturize your dry lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue felicia tea helps with dry lips because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to soothe and heal dry, chapped lips by providing essential moisture and protecting the lips from further damage. Additionally, blue felicia tea is rich in vitamins and minerals that help to nourish and rejuvenate the lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. The tea's natural emollient properties also help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort. Drinking blue felicia tea or using it as a lip balm can provide effective relief from dry lips and promote healthy, moisturized lips.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Blue felicia tea contains high levels of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by dry, cracked lips.
Antimicrobial: Blue felicia tea has natural antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing of dry, damaged lips.
Exfoliating: The tea contains gentle exfoliating properties, which can help remove dead skin cells and promote the growth of new, healthy lip cells.
Soothing: Blue felicia tea has a calming effect on the skin, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it an effective remedy for dry, irritated lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and essential oils that help to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation caused by dry lips.
Buds: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants that can help to nourish and protect the lips from environmental stressors that can cause dryness.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that can help to lock in moisture and keep lips hydrated for longer periods of time.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried blue felicia flowers.
Steep flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain out flowers, leaving only liquid.
Allow tea to cool to a comfortable temperature.
Sip tea slowly, letting it moisturize your dry lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with dry lips because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has moisturizing properties. This compound helps to soothe and hydrate the lips, providing relief from dryness and cracking. Additionally, licorice tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the lips from environmental stressors that can contribute to dryness. Drinking licorice tea or using it as a lip rinse can help to keep the lips soft, supple, and moisturized. However, it is important to note that consuming large amounts of licorice root can have side effects, so it is best to use licorice tea in moderation.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Licorice tea has a demulcent property that creates a protective film on the lips, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. This property is due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and other polysaccharides in licorice root.

Humectant property: Licorice tea acts as a humectant, drawing and retaining moisture in the lips. This is beneficial for dry lips as it helps to keep them hydrated and supple. The sugars and other organic compounds in licorice contribute to its humectant properties.

Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation on the lips. This is particularly helpful for dry lips, as the tea can alleviate discomfort caused by dryness and chapping. The flavonoids and isoflavonoids in licorice are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antioxidant property: Licorice tea contains antioxidants that protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and aging. This property helps maintain lip health and prevents further dryness. The flavonoids and isoflavonoids, as well as other phenolic compounds in licorice, contribute to its antioxidant properties.

Emollient property: Licorice tea has emollient properties that help soften and smooth the lips, providing relief from dryness and roughness. This property is due to the essential oils, waxes, and fatty acids present in licorice root.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which helps to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and antioxidants that can help protect and nourish the lips, reducing dryness and cracking.
Flowers: High in essential oils and antioxidants, promoting healthy lip tissue and providing soothing relief for dry, irritated lips.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients and fatty acids, seeds can help to lock in moisture and protect lips from environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to moisturize your lips from the inside.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with dry lips because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to moisturize and soothe chapped lips. The tea is also rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote healing. Drinking witch hazel tea can help to hydrate the body from the inside out, while applying a cool compress soaked in witch hazel tea to the lips can provide immediate relief and help to lock in moisture. Additionally, the tea can help to reduce redness and inflammation, leaving the lips looking and feeling soft and supple.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea can reduce inflammation, which is important for dry lips as inflammation can make lips more sensitive and prone to cracking. This property is derived from tannins in witch hazel.
Astringent: Witch hazel tea has astringent properties that tighten and protect skin, helping to prevent moisture loss from dry lips. This is also due to the presence of tannins.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea contains antioxidants that help protect skin from damage, which is crucial for dry lips as they are more susceptible to environmental stressors. The antioxidant effects come from flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Emollient: Witch hazel tea can help soothe and soften dry, rough lips, providing relief from symptoms of dryness. This emollient effect is attributed to saponins and essential oils in witch hazel.

Antimicrobial: Witch hazel tea has antimicrobial properties that can help keep lips clean and free from infection. This is important for dry lips as they can be more prone to irritation and infection. The antimicrobial properties are primarily due to the presence of gallic acid and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins, leaves help soothe and heal dry, chapped lips.
Bark: Contains anti-inflammatory properties, bark helps reduce redness and irritation on dry lips.
Flowers: High in antioxidants, flowers help protect lips from environmental stressors causing dryness.
Buds: Packed with nutrients, buds promote cell regeneration and hydration for dry lips.
Rhizomes: Full of essential oils, rhizomes provide a soothing effect and help lock in moisture.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top kettle, boil 2 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried witch hazel bark to a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the witch hazel bark and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a mug, discarding the spent herbs.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe and moisturize your dry lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy tea helps with dry lips because it contains moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate chapped lips. The plant is rich in saponins, which have emollient qualities that can help lock in moisture and protect the lips from environmental stressors. Additionally, English ivy has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce redness, irritation, and inflammation, promoting healthy and supple lips. Drinking English ivy tea can also help keep the body hydrated, which can contribute to overall lip health. However, it is important to note that while English ivy tea can be beneficial for dry lips, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment for severe or chronic lip conditions.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: English ivy tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective film over the lips, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dryness.
Anti-inflammatory: English ivy tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of saponins and flavonoids, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation on dry lips.
Antioxidant: English ivy tea contains antioxidants, such as rutin and quercetin, which can help protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote healing of dry, chapped lips.
Antimicrobial: English ivy tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote wound healing, making it an effective treatment for dry, cracked lips.
Circulation-boosting: English ivy tea contains compounds that can help improve blood flow, which can promote healthy lip tissue and speed up the healing process for dry, damaged lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy tea plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for dry lips due to their ability to moisturize and soothe chapped lips.
Stems: The stems of the English ivy tea plant contain beneficial compounds that can help to heal and protect dry, cracked lips.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy tea plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to nourish and repair dry, damaged lips.
Bark: The bark of the English ivy tea plant contains tannins and other compounds that can help to soothe and protect dry, irritated lips.
Flowers: The flowers of the English ivy tea plant contain essential oils and other compounds that can help to moisturize and soften dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Pick three to four English ivy leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and healthy.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and potential contaminants.
Crush the leaves gently to release their natural oils and active compounds.
Steep the crushed leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and allow it to cool before applying to dry lips using a cotton swab.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry lips with herbal teas is to steep a tea bag in hot water for several minutes, then allow the tea to cool. Gently apply the cooled tea to your lips using a cotton ball or clean finger. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in the tea will help to soothe and heal dry, chapped lips. Chamomile and lavender teas are particularly beneficial for their moisturizing and calming effects. Repeat this process several times a day for best results. Additionally, be sure to stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry lips that are treated with herbal teas include chapped and inflamed skin. Certain herbal teas have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips and skin. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling, while rosehip tea is rich in vitamins and antioxidants that help moisturize and rejuvenate dry, damaged skin. To use, brew a strong cup of tea, let it cool, and apply it to the affected area using a cotton ball or spray bottle. However, it is always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies to treat any ailment.
